projects.name: Hosts
projects.description: POSIX-compliant shell script that gets a list of domains that serve ads, tracking scripts and malware from multiple sources and creates a hosts file, that prevents your system from connecting to them. Based on hBlock, but with a lot of my curated lists and personalized blocking.
projects.short_description: Curated hosts file with various domain blocks

projects.name: FOSS-Q
projects.description: A metasearch engine which retrieves search results from other well known search engines and then strips away all of the tracking parameters to give you a clean and private result.
projects.short_description: Fast, privacy-first search for open source users

projects.name: LibreY
projects.description: LibreY is a fork of LibreX, a framework-less and javascript-free privacy respecting meta search engine originally created by hnhx, and now maintained by Ahwxorg. This is my instance.
projects.short_description: Magnet link and general search in one place

projects.name: LibreQR
projects.description: PHP Web interface for generating QR codes. Liberated & fully awesome.
projects.short_description: Generate QR & Wi-Fi QR codes instantly

projects.name: Paste
projects.description: Secure, encrypted pastes for sensitive texts.

projects.name: Git
projects.description: My public instance of the Forgejo version control system. I have no other words to describe, how cool it is.
projects.short_description: Host and manage your open source projects with Forgejo

form.question: How to support
form.response: I am looking for opportunities to network and share my projects to a broader audience so they can be utilized effectively (Note - bots will be restricted). I would greatly appreciate any feedback or suggestions. Additionally, I welcome donations to help support the ongoing operations and maintenance of the servers.

form.question: A small brief about your project
form.response: FLOSSbOxIN was started in 2021 with the primary goal of promoting independence from bigger conglomerates, as much as possible. The project currently maintains the above-mentioned instances for users, with infrastructure hosted in Germany.

form.question: One FOSS maintainer lesson for your younger self
form.response: I may not fully meet the criteria, but I believe this moment will not last forever - making it important to act now rather than wait.

form.question: Why do you do it? Why do you bother maintaining a FOSS project?
form.response: Honestly, my motivation is to enjoy my freedom, my privacy and my data. It has helped me learn a lot - and I continue to learn every day. It allows me to do meaningful work and, more importantly, to give back to the community. My motto is - My data is my own, and it is no one else’s business. Everyone deserves access to FOSS/FLOSS projects and apps, and I am grateful for the opportunity to help make that possible.

form.question: If your repo had a theme song, what would it be?
form.response: Mabel – Disco Disco (Paolo Ferrali; Italian Euro House)

form.question: If you had to use one emoji to convey what it is like to be a FOSS maintainer, what would it be?
form.response: The Raising Hands 🙌 that expresses my excitement Or the Upside-Down Smiley Face 🙃 that shows happy to be a part of it.
